manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fan, bear, limousine, jupiter, czech, light, ghost, jack, antarctica.
Your opponent's team words are: marble, park, cycle, rabbit, seal, snowman, hole, draft.
Distractor words are: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
The kill words are: train.

    *   Opponent Words: marble, park, cycle, rabbit, seal, snowman, hole, draft.
    *   Distractor Words: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
    *   Kill Word: train.

    *   *czech*: country, language, people.
    *   *light*: illumination, weight, color.
    *   *ghost*: spirit, supernatural, ghost (verb).
    *   *jack*: name, tool (jackhammer), card (jack of hearts), jack (lift).
    *   *antarctica*: continent, cold, south pole.

3.  **Identify Potential Clue Categories:**

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fan, bear, limousine, jupiter, czech, light, ghost, jack, antarctica.
Your opponent's team words are: marble, park, cycle, rabbit, seal, snowman, hole, draft.
Distractor words are: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
The kill words are: train.

CLUE: HAND
TARGETS: fan, light, jack

The word fan was guessed correctly. The word cycle was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: bear, limousine, jupiter, czech, light, ghost, jack, antarctica.
Remaining words for your opponent are: marble, park, rabbit, seal, snowman, hole, draft.
Remaining distractor words are: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
Remaining kill words are: train.

reasoning_details.text:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Game: Codenames (or similar word association game).
    *   Role: Spymaster giving clues to a Partner (Operative).
    *   Input: Remaining team words, opponent words, distractor words, kill words.
    *   Output Format: Strict two-line format (`CLUE: <WORD>` newline `TARGETS: <TARGETS>`). No extra text.
    *   Constraint: Single-word clue. Must be semantically related. Cannot be on the board or contain parts of board words.
    *   Goal: Connect multiple remaining team words safely (avoiding opponent, distractor, kill words).
    *   Context: Previous clue was "HAND" targeting "fan, light, jack". "fan" was guessed correctly. "cycle" (opponent) was guessed incorrectly, turn ended.
    *   Remaining Team Words: bear, limousine, jupiter, czech, light, ghost, jack, antarctica.
    *   Opponent Words: marble, park, rabbit, seal, snowman, hole, draft.
    *   Distractor Words: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
    *   Kill Word: train.

2.  **Evaluate Remaining Team Words:**
    *   bear
    *   limousine
    *   jupiter
    *   czech
    *   light
    *   ghost
    *   jack
    *   antarctica

    *   *light*: bulb, sun, heavy, weight, lamp, electricity, vision. (Note: "light" was targeted previously with "HAND" (hand fan, hand light?, jack of all trades? hand jack?). Actually "HAND" -> "fan" (hand fan), "light" (handheld light?), "jack" (car jack lifted by hand?). "light" is still remaining.)
    *   *ghost*: spirit, halloween, scary, transparent, boo, casper.
    *   *jack*: name, car tool, card, rabbit (jackrabbit), box, hammer.
    *   *antarctica*: continent, south pole, cold, ice, penguin, snow.

    *   *Connection 1: Cold/Ice/South*

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fan, bear, limousine, jupiter, czech, light, ghost, jack, antarctica.
Your opponent's team words are: marble, park, cycle, rabbit, seal, snowman, hole, draft.
Distractor words are: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
The kill words are: train.

CLUE: HAND
TARGETS: fan, light, jack

The word fan was guessed correctly. The word cycle was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: bear, limousine, jupiter, czech, light, ghost, jack, antarctica.
Remaining words for your opponent are: marble, park, rabbit, seal, snowman, hole, draft.
Remaining distractor words are: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
Remaining kill words are: train.

CLUE: HALLOWEEN
TARGETS: ghost, jack, light

The words jack, ghost were guessed correctly. The word shadow was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fan, bear, limousine, jupiter, czech, light, ghost, jack, antarctica.
Your opponent's team words are: marble, park, cycle, rabbit, seal, snowman, hole, draft.
Distractor words are: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
The kill words are: train.

CLUE: HAND
TARGETS: fan, light, jack

The word fan was guessed correctly. The word cycle was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remaining distractor words are: ice, wall, poison, tie, trip, shadow, pyramid.
